pain_points: Ensuring confidentiality and integrity of patient data
Mitigating risks of data breaches and cyber attacks
Protecting sensitive patient information from unauthorized access
regulatory_drivers: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) for international patients
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA)
Indian Medical Council (Professional Conduct, Etiquette and Ethics) Regulations, 2002
environment_assumptions: Cloud-based infrastructure for data storage and processing
Use of mobile and web applications for patient engagement
Integration with existing healthcare systems and networks
Presence of IoT devices for patient monitoring
discovery_questions: What types of patient data will be collected, stored, and processed?
How will data be protected in transit and at rest?
What measures are in place for access control and authentication?
How will data breaches be detected and responded to?
What is the incident response plan in case of a security incident?
urgency_signals: Increasing regulatory scrutiny in the healthcare industry
Recent data breaches in similar organizations
Growing concerns about patient data privacy
tier_rationale: The recommended tier is Enterprise due to the high-risk nature of processing sensitive patient data and the need for comprehensive data protection measures to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
